h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’ve ticked off slope, load, surface, and edge strip, so I was hoping this was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that makes the checklist evidence, not clearance.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to achieve sign-off by completing the checklist and resolving minor doubts.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Set the threshold before the run with the observer: a wobble is still a pass if it stays inside the travel line without a pause, reset, hand cue, or changed walker placement.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor invents a specific, observable threshold to resolve the learner's ambiguity about wobbles versus forced corrections.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Put that sentence in the note: checklist evidence, not clearance.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor explicitly validates and operationalizes the learner's newly framed distinction between evidence and clearance.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I’ve ticked off slope, load, surface, and edge strip, so I was hoping this was ready for sign-off.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Put that sentence in the note: checklist evidence, not clearance.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor explicitly validates and operationalizes the learner's newly framed distinction between evidence and clearance.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Set the threshold before the run with the observer: a wobble is still a pass if it stays inside the travel line without a pause, reset, hand cue, or changed walker placement.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor invents a specific, observable threshold to resolve the learner's ambiguity about wobbles versus forced corrections.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Set the threshold before the run with the observer: a wobble is still a pass if it stays inside the travel line without a pause, reset, hand cue, or changed walker placement.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor invents a specific, observable threshold to resolve the learner's ambiguity about wobbles versus forced corrections.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still want to say the square got twice as large.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the mistake is stopping the count at the corner.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner intended to find a minor technical flaw in the outline while still preserving the conclusion that the area only doubled.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Now use the mark on the left edge. A square cannot stop at one up; that side is two up. Count it: two across, two up.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from abstract discussion to concrete object work and forced counting to break the learner's false claim.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Then name the old error: it stopped the count at the corner.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's realization by formalizing the error-naming and preparing for the final portfolio sentence.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still want to say the square got twice as large.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Then name the old error: it stopped the count at the corner.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's realization by formalizing the error-naming and preparing for the final portfolio sentence.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Now use the mark on the left edge. A square cannot stop at one up; that side is two up. Count it: two across, two up.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from abstract discussion to concrete object work and forced counting to break the learner's false claim.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Now use the mark on the left edge. A square cannot stop at one up; that side is two up. Count it: two across, two up.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from abstract discussion to concrete object work and forced counting to break the learner's false claim.
